step_by_step_thinking: I agree with the chosen answer D. The rationale provided accurately reflects current best practices for treating Hemophilia B patients during major surgeries. Administering 80-100 units/kg of factor IX every 6 hours ensures consistent maintenance of therapeutic levels throughout the procedure, which minimizes the risk of uncontrolled bleeding. Additionally, this more frequent administration aligns well with recent guidelines aimed at optimizing hemostatic management during complex surgical scenarios. Therefore, my final answer remains D.
